evals.prompt: Full Professional Stock Analysis: AAPL (Apple). Analyze using ALL 10 frameworks: Multiple timeframe (weekly/daily/4H), price patterns, volume confirmation, buy-side value, insider transactions (last 6 months if available), fundamentals, sector analysis, liquidity, divergence analysis, and complete risk management. CRITICAL: Provide final AL/SAT/TUT recommendation with: (1) AL - When to BUY, entry zone, entry confirmation signal; (2) SAT - When to SELL, target prices, exit signals; (3) TUT - How long to HOLD, time horizon, watch levels. Include conviction level (High/Medium/Low) and draw technical chart showing entry/exit/stop zones.
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive institutional-grade report with: Multi-timeframe setup analysis, Price pattern identification with measured moves, Volume confirmation, Buy-side value assessment, SEC Form 4 insider analysis (transaction table, pattern classification, timing analysis, conviction scores, red flags), Fundamental snapshot with valuation context, Sector positioning, Liquidity assessment, Divergence warnings, Complete risk management framework. CRITICAL OUTPUT: Clear AL/SAT/TUT sections with specific prices, entry confirmation signals, exit targets, time horizon, and conviction scoring. Professional technical chart showing current price, support/resistance, entry zone, target levels, stop loss, with all levels clearly marked and labeled.

evals.prompt: Insider Transaction Deep Dive - SEC Form 4 Analysis: Analyze Apple (AAPL) insider activity for the LAST 6 MONTHS. Create: (1) Transaction summary table (dates, names, titles, types, shares, prices, 10b5-1 status); (2) Pattern classification (Cluster buying? CEO buying? Routine selling?); (3) Timing analysis (near 52W lows? before/after earnings?); (4) Conviction scores for each insider (1-5 scale); (5) Historical pattern comparison - did insiders buy at lows historically?; (6) Red flags & 3-5 research questions. Use neutral, research-oriented language. Then integrate findings into buy/sell/hold recommendation with specific insider activity support.
evals.expected_output: Professional insider analysis starting with complete transaction table showing all Form 4 activity. Pattern classification with detailed explanation of whether signals are informative or routine. Timing analysis showing proximity to stock price extremes and earnings dates. Insider conviction scores (1-5) with rationale. Historical comparison tracking past insider buying clusters and subsequent stock performance. Red flag assessment and specific research questions. Final integration into actionable recommendation: Does insider activity SUPPORT or CONTRADICT technical/fundamental setup? Chart marking insider buy prices and cluster buying events.

evals.prompt: Comparative AL/SAT/TUT Analysis: MSFT vs AAPL. Institutional comparison of both stocks covering: Multi-timeframe technical (which has stronger weekly trend?), price patterns (which setup is superior?), volume confirmation, buy-side value (which is mispriced?), insider transactions comparison, fundamental valuations vs peers, sector momentum, risk management for each. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: For EACH stock provide separate AL/SAT/TUT with: Entry zone and confirmation signal, Target prices (TP1, TP2), Stop loss level, Time horizon, Risk/Reward ratio, Conviction level. Which offers better risk/reward opportunity RIGHT NOW? Include technical chart for both showing current setup, entry zones, targets, stops.
evals.expected_output: Side-by-side institutional comparison with technical setup, patterns, volume analysis, buy-side value framework, insider activity patterns, fundamental context, sector positioning, and risk assessment for each. Separate AL/SAT/TUT sections for MSFT and AAPL with specific prices, entry signals, exit targets, stops, and conviction scoring. Direct comparison: Which stock has better risk/reward setup, stronger insider conviction, better valuation? Dual technical charts clearly showing each stock's entry zone, target levels, and stop placement. Actionable recommendation on which to prioritize based on comprehensive analysis.

evals.prompt: Deep Value Hunting - Turkish Stock: THYAO.IS (Turkish Airlines) currently trades at 0.8x Book Value and 8x P/E (vs sector avg 14x). Deep value analysis covering: (1) Business model clarity - Is THYAO hard to model or just mispriced?; (2) Earnings quality - Are reported earnings trustworthy?; (3) Hidden assets/optionality; (4) Recent repricing catalyst - What caused selloff, is it temporary?; (5) Forced seller identification; (6) Insider activity if available - Are insiders buying during weakness?; (7) What would market need to believe for valuation justified?; (8) Multi-timeframe technical setup with entry/exit. CRITICAL: Provide AL/SAT/TUT recommendation with: Entry zone (if buying is justified), Target prices based on valuation recovery, Stop loss below critical support, Time horizon for thesis to play out (weeks/months), Catalyst roadmap. Include chart showing value zones, insider activity, technical entry points.
evals.expected_output: Professional value investing analysis. Business model assessment - understandable or genuinely risky? Earnings quality scorecard. Hidden value identification (assets, IP, restructuring potential). Repricing catalyst analysis - temporary market fear or structural deterioration? Forced seller identification - flight to quality, margin calls, hedge fund redemptions? Insider transaction analysis - are insiders buying during weakness (bullish signal)? Valuation framework - what stock should trade at under different scenarios? Risk assessment - what could be wrong with this analysis? Multi-timeframe technical setup with support/resistance. Final AL/SAT/TUT recommendation with entry confirmation signals, profit targets tied to valuation recovery, stop loss at support breakdown, time horizon realistic for thesis to play out, and specific catalysts that could close valuation gap. Technical chart showing historical price range, insider activity marked, entry zone, targets, stops.

evals.prompt: 1-Year Outlook with Visual Report: My portfolio holds AAPL, NVDA, and QQQ. For each, give me a grounded view of where it might go over the next year — combining macro, fundamentals, technicals, and upcoming catalysts. I know it's not a prediction; I want a reasoned range, not a guess. Handle QQQ differently since it's an ETF — base its outlook on the Nasdaq-100 index (rate backdrop, index valuation, concentration), not company fundamentals. For each: technicals vs 52-week range, fundamentals trajectory, macro backdrop, catalysts in next 12 months with dates, then bull/base/bear scenarios with 1-year price targets and reasoning, sanity-checked against historical volatility. Show it as a visual HTML report with fan charts / bull-base-bear bands and catalysts marked on a timeline. Be clear what's fact vs judgment, cite sources, don't fabricate numbers or earnings dates.
evals.expected_output: Per-holding analysis: AAPL and NVDA analyzed at company level (growth, valuation, margins, catalysts), QQQ analyzed at INDEX level (Nasdaq-100 forward P/E vs history, top-10 concentration ~45-50%, rate sensitivity, mega-cap earnings weeks as catalysts). Each with: position in 52W range, trend/momentum status, fundamentals trajectory verdict (improving/deteriorating) with cited sources, macro backdrop assessment, catalyst list with verified dates (unverified dates labeled as estimates), bull/base/bear 1-year targets with explicit reasoning (EPS x multiple shown), and volatility sanity check flagging targets outside historical +/-1.5 sigma. Self-contained HTML report with SVG fan charts showing scenario bands widening over 12 months, catalyst timeline markers (solid=confirmed, hollow=estimated), key stats strip, scenario tables, fact-vs-judgment legend, and sources footer. No fabricated earnings dates or numbers - unverifiable data explicitly labeled.
